LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY UDELL DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO DEFENSES 1 AND 3-35 IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY UDELL DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT

Plaintiff's demurrer to defenses 1 and 3-35 is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

(see word minutes for complete details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Continued To Apr-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. NATHAN SEARCY, Esq. of Webber & Egbert Employment Law, P.C. (tel: 916-588-0683) for Plaintiff.
Atty. RONAK PATEL, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (tel: 925-357-3456) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JEFFREY UDELL's DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed as to the meet and confer efforts. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. Meet and confer efforts are insufficient under CCP Section 435.5. Counsel exchanged meet and confer letters instead of meeting and conferring "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" as required.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)
